audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra priestly holy-food and offering acceptability text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for priestly holy-food boundaries, temporary impurity, household eligibility, hired worker/sojourner distinctions, acceptable offerings, animal defects, mother-and-young rule, thanksgiving timing, and sanctification refrain.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to Aaron and to his sons, that they keep separate from holy things of children of Israel, and not profane my holy name in what they sanctify to me. I am YHWH.

Say to them: Throughout your generations, any man from all your seed who draws near to holy things that children of Israel sanctify to YHWH, while his uncleanness is upon him, that soul/life shall be cut off from before me. I am YHWH.

Any man from seed of Aaron who is afflicted with scale-disease or has discharge shall not eat from holy things until he is clean. Whoever touches any unclean by dead soul/life, or man from whom emission of seed goes out,
or man who touches any swarming thing by which he becomes unclean, or human by whom he becomes unclean, whatever his uncleanness,
the soul/life touching it shall be unclean until evening and shall not eat from holy things unless he washes his flesh in water.
When sun goes down, he shall be clean, and afterward he may eat from holy things, for it is his food.
Carcass and torn animal he shall not eat to become unclean by it. I am YHWH.
They shall keep my charge, lest they bear sin/misalignment because of it and die in it if they profane it. I am YHWH who sanctifies them.
No stranger shall eat holy thing; resident guest of priest or hired worker shall not eat holy thing.
But when priest buys a soul/life, acquisition of his silver, that one may eat of it, and one born in his house; they may eat from his food.
If daughter of priest belongs to a strange man, she shall not eat from contribution of holy things.
But if daughter of priest becomes widow or divorced and has no seed, and returns to house of her father as in her youth, she may eat from food of her father; but no stranger shall eat of it.
If a man eats holy thing in error/unintentionally, he shall add fifth to it and give holy thing to priest.
They shall not profane holy things of children of Israel, which they lift to YHWH,
and so cause them to bear iniquity of guilt/reparation when they eat their holy things; for I am YHWH who sanctifies them.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

Speak to Aaron and his sons and to all children of Israel, and say to them: Any man from house of Israel or from sojourners in Israel who brings near his offering, for any vows or any freewill offerings that they bring near to YHWH for burnt offering,
for your acceptance it shall be complete male from herd, sheep, or goats.
Anything in which there is bodily blemish you shall not bring near, for it shall not be for acceptance for you.

When a man brings near sacrifice of peace/well-being offerings to YHWH to fulfill a vow or for freewill offering from herd or flock, it shall be complete for acceptance; no bodily blemish shall be in it.
Blind, broken, maimed, having discharge/wart, scab, or skin eruption—you shall not bring these near to YHWH, and fire-offering you shall not give from them on altar to YHWH.
Ox or lamb with limb too long or too short you may make freewill offering, but for vow it shall not be accepted.
Crushed, bruised, torn, or cut you shall not bring near to YHWH; in your land you shall not do this.
From hand of foreigner you shall not bring bread/food of your Divine from any of these, because their corruption is in them, bodily blemish is in them; they shall not be accepted for you.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
When ox or sheep or goat is born, it shall be seven days under its mother, and from eighth day onward it shall be accepted as offering, fire-offering to YHWH.
Ox or sheep, it and its young you shall not slaughter in one day.
When you sacrifice thanksgiving offering to YHWH, for your acceptance you shall sacrifice.
On that day it shall be eaten; you shall leave none of it until morning. I am YHWH.
You shall keep my commandments and do them. I am YHWH.
You shall not profane my holy name, and I will be sanctified in midst of children of Israel. I am YHWH who sanctifies you,
who brought you out from land of Egypt to be to you as Divine. I am YHWH.

translation_notes: Priestly holy-food rules are ritual-boundary rules around food dedicated to YHWH, not human worth ranking.
Temporary impurity resolves by washing and sunset; v.7 confirms return to holy food because it is priestly food.
Household eligibility reflects ancient household economy and requires anti-slavery/gender audit.
Offering blemish restrictions concern ritual acceptability of sacrificial animals, not a theology that disabled bodies are inferior.
Mother-and-young one-day slaughter limit and eighth-day animal rule should be preserved as animal-life boundary.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 22 to shame disabled people, illness, poverty, foreigners, women, or household status. The chapter regulates priestly holy food and sacrificial acceptability within an ancient ritual system, while ending with Exodus liberation memory.
